records.ety: [Prob. ultimately fr. Heb. arabah a desert, the name employed, in the Old Testament, to denote the valley of the Jordan and Dead Sea. Ar. Arab, Heb. arabi, arbi, arbim: cf. F. Arabe, L. Arabs, Gr. <?/.]

defs: One of a swarthy race occupying Arabia, and numerous in Syria, Northern Africa, etc.
quotes: The ragged outcasts and street Arabs who are shivering in damp doorways.
